get.description: This endpoint returns the transcript from the video from a lesson

parameters.name: lesson
parameters.description: The slug of the lesson

get.description: Search for a term and find lessons that contain similar text in their video transcripts

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: A snippet of text to search for in the lesson video transcripts

get.description: This endpoint returns signed download URLs and types for the assets currently available on Oak for a given sequence

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Use the this type and the lesson slug in conjunction to get a signed download URL to the asset type from the /api/lessons/{slug}/asset/{type} endpoint

get.description: This endpoint returns signed download URLs and types for the assets currently available on Oak for a given key stage and subject, optionally filtered by type and unit, grouped by lesson

parameters.name: keyStage
parameters.description: Key stage slug to filter by, e.g. 'ks2' - note that casing is important here, and should be lowercase

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Subject slug to search by, e.g. 'science' - note that casing is important here (always lowercase)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Use the this type and the lesson slug in conjunction to get a signed download URL to the asset type from the /api/lessons/{slug}/asset/{type} endpoint

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: Optional unit slug to additionally filter by

get.description: This endpoint returns signed download URLS and types for the assets currently available on Oak for a given lesson

parameters.name: lesson
parameters.description: The lesson slug

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Use the this type and the lesson slug in conjunction to get a signed download URL to the asset type from the /api/lessons/{slug}/asset/{type} endpoint

get.description: This endpoint will stream the downloadable asset for the given lesson and type

parameters.name: lesson
parameters.description: The lesson slug

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Use the this type and the lesson slug in conjunction to get a signed download URL to the asset type from the /api/lessons/{slug}/asset/{type} endpoint

get.description: This endpoint returns an array of all subjects and associated sequences, key stages and years that are currently available on Oak
This endpoint returns a single subject and associated sequences, key stages and years.
List of the sequences, including phase, key stage 4 options, years and key stages the sequence applies to for a subject.
List of the key stages a subject is taught in.
List of the years a subject is taught in.
This endpoint returns all the key stages (titles and slugs) that are currently available on Oak
This endpoint returns all the lessons (titles and slugs) that are currently available on Oak for a given subject and key stage, grouped by unit

parameters.name: keyStage
parameters.description: Key stage slug to filter by, e.g. 'ks2' - note that casing is important here, and should be lowercase

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Subject slug to filter by, e.g. 'english' - note that casing is important here, and should be lowercase

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: Optional unit slug to additionally filter by

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of results returned, max 100

get.description: This endpoint returns all the units (titles and slugs) that are currently available on Oak for a given subject and key stage

parameters.name: keyStage
parameters.description: Key stage slug to filter by, e.g. 'ks2'

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Subject slug to search by, e.g. 'science' - note that casing is important here (always lowercase)

get.description: The endpoint returns the quiz questions and answers for a given lesson. The answers data indicates which answers are correct, and which are distractors.
This endpoint returns the quiz questions and answers (and indicates which answers are correct and which are distractors) for a given sequence

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of results returned, max 100

get.description: This endpoint returns all the quiz questions and answers (and indicates which answers are correct and which are distractors), grouped by lesson, for a given key stage and subject

parameters.name: keyStage
parameters.description: Key stage slug to filter by, e.g. 'ks2' - note that casing is important here, and should be lowercase

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Subject slug to search by, e.g. 'science' - note that casing is important here

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of results returned, max 100

get.description: This endpoint returns a summary for a given lesson

parameters.name: lesson
parameters.description: The slug of the lesson

get.description: This endpoint returns lessons that are similar to the search criteria, including a similarity score, and details of the unit that it is in

parameters.name: keyStage
parameters.description: Key stage slug to filter by, e.g. 'ks2' - note that casing is important here, and should be lowercase

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Subject slug to filter by, e.g. 'english' - note that casing is important here, and should be lowercase

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: Optional unit slug to additionally filter by

get.description: This endpoint returns unit information for a given unit, including slug, title, number of lessons, prior knowledge requirements, national curriculum statements, prior unit details, future unit descriptions, and lesson titles that form the unit

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: The unit slug

get.description: Get all threads that can be used as sequence filters.
Get all units for a specific thread filter.

changes: All video assets now fully downloadable in mp4 format
Added /sequences/* and /subjects/* endpoints, and add support for unit optionality

get.description: Get the latest version and latest change note for the API

changes: All video assets now fully downloadable in mp4 format

get.description:
Check your current rate limit status (note that your rate limit is also included in the headers of every response).

This specific endpoint does not cost any requests.

A 2D shape made of 3D shapes folded togehther.

example.lessonTitle: Predicting the size of a product

starterQuiz.question: Match the number to its written representation.

exitQuiz.question: Use the fact that 9 × 8 = 72, to match the expressions to their product.

lessonKeywords.description: a word that joins words or ideas

keyLearningPoints.keyLearningPoint: And is a type of joining word.
A joining word can join two simple sentences.
Each simple sentence is about one idea and makes complete sense.
The second idea builds on to the first idea if ‘and’ is used to join them.
Grammatically accurate sentences start with capital letters and most often end with full stops.

misconceptionsAndCommonMistakes.misconception: Pupils may struggle to link related ideas together.
misconceptionsAndCommonMistakes.response: Give some non-examples to show what it sounds like when two ideas are unrelated e.g. Dad baked bread and she missed her sister.

example.pupilLessonOutcome: I can join two simple sentences with 'and'.

teacherTips.teacherTip: In Learning Cycle 1, make sure pupils are given plenty of opportunities to say sentences orally and hear that they make complete sense.

units.unitTitle: A monster within: reading and writing Gothic fiction

example.lessonTitle: 'The Twisted Tree': the novel as a Gothic text

example.unitTitle: Simple, compound and adverbial complex sentences
priorKnowledgeRequirements: A simple sentence is about one idea and makes complete sense.
Any simple sentence contains one verb and at least one noun.
Two simple sentences can be joined with a co-ordinating conjunction to form a compound sentence.
nationalCurriculumContent: Ask relevant questions to extend their understanding and knowledge
Articulate and justify answers, arguments and opinions
Speak audibly and fluently with an increasing command of Standard English
